# Transcrição: 08. Priming Your Facebook Page

- **Fonte:** 08. Priming Your Facebook Page
- **Duração:** 05:34
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:04]

Okay, so I quickly want to talk to you about your Facebook page. Now the reason I'm putting this here is because it's not terribly important that you go and create a whole bunch of content for all of these social platforms. I certainly never had that when I was doing my freelancing business or for that matter scaling up my agency. However, it will help you when you're ready to automate your prospecting or your marketing. What I mean by that is Facebook is now looking at the activity on your Facebook page and it's taking that into account in the auction process. So in the incubator we're looking at prospecting techniques which involve leveraging our positioning and leveraging our competence and reaching out to prospects. When we're inside the agency incubator we're automating that through paid traffic channels and Facebook will definitely give you much better results if you have some engagement and some content on here. So in the messaging document that you would have just seen in the previous section you saw at the bottom there was a sort of content pillars table here. Now let me talk to you guys a little bit about this and then I'll explain how to run this on your Facebook page in the most effective way. So basically people need to know, like and trust you before they're going to buy from you right? And so this is affinity, awareness and authority, the three A's. Underneath each of these I've put sort of types of content that you can put underneath them to help build that and really what you do is you just basically post on your page and you rotate through these pillars. It doesn't have to be perfect, you don't have to go affinity, awareness, authority, affinity, awareness, authority all the time. You can swap and change but the aim is that you're not just posting affinity, you're not just posting memes, you're not just posting, hey, buy my stuff, you're not just posting case studies all the time. You're rotating through that and really establishing a connection with your potential leads and reaffirming your positioning really. So for example under authority you could have case studies, testimonials, industry tips, et cetera, et cetera. Awards would even go in there if you have that to place in there. Now what you do is when you've created your Facebook page and you want to start that process now if you don't have one already because again as I mentioned eventually you may want to drive traffic into a funnel and automate that side of your business and you really want to set it up so that you get the best traction from this. So once you create your page I would recommend that you aim to get at least 170, 200 likes on that page to give it enough credibility. You can run a page like campaign for that. You can also invite your close friends and family. I wouldn't send out a bulk invite to everybody but certainly your close friends and family.

## [03:04 - 05:34]

And then what you do is just once a week go and post a piece of content that rotates through this pillar so you can almost set up all of your content marketing for the month in half an afternoon because you're essentially only looking for four pieces of content. What you're going to do when you post it on your Facebook page is you're going to boost the content with about five dollars so it's not much. So you're spending twenty dollars a month on this. You're going to boost it to an audience that makes sense for you or your existing page fans but you want to get some traction. What you don't want to see is have a post with zero likes, zero comments, zero share, zero engagement. It's not going to establish a lot of trust for potential leads that are looking to work with you. And then what you're going to do is just keep that going and when people comment just engage, just moderate the comments. That's really important. That's what Facebook is looking at. Now inside of the insights that you have here what you can also do is have a look at your posts once you've posted those and put some data behind it and you'll be able to see the engagement that the posts have had and you can also dig down and see the negative feedback, etc., etc. If you edit this you can see the engagement rate right here which is easiest. So you can see here for example this type of post had one of the highest engagement rates and this will help you to refine the marketing that you do or the content that you do. For example this was tips that had the highest engagement rate and it was a video. So going forward I know that videos with tips are really high engagement pieces of content for my page and Facebook is really going to like that and it works really well. I get shown to more people and so I'm going to adjust my marketing strategy to make sure that I include a lot more of that. The point being here that you want to start being active on your Facebook page and you want to make sure that you put a bit of budget behind the content that you're putting out and I'm not asking you to put out much, just four posts per month really. It could be three but you're basically rotating through these and you're going to put a budget behind them so that you can actually get some engagement from them and this will help you when you're ready to automate your prospecting. Again the incubator is all about setting a foundation for a really successful and sustainable business that will be around for a while and this is part of that. So while you may not see the returns from this right away it definitely is worth implementing immediately.
